_doc: Deny-only rails for ACE, enforced by hooks/gating_guard.py (plugin-level PreToolUse — fires in every session with ACE installed, so rules stay NARROW). deny = hard block (exit 2) with a message naming the right path; the agent self-corrects and keeps going, so rails cost autonomy nothing. There are deliberately NO approve/ask rules: interactive permission prompts stall autonomous runs, and ACE governs its outbound moments procedurally instead (pause points + mode matrix in run_state.yaml; review-posture in turns; solicitation-review's HITL checkpoint before award_response). Decision: Jon, 2026-07-01 — hooks are rails, not approval gates. 'pattern' is regex on the Bash command / Edit-Write file_path / compact-JSON MCP input; 'tool_pattern' is regex on the tool name. Rationale: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-agent-operating-model-adoption.md.

deny.message: BLOCKED: raw `gog gmail send/reply` under the ACE identity. Sends go ONLY through `bin/ace-email` (HTML multipart + comms-log threadId capture; --dry-run to preview). See skills/email-communicator/SKILL.md.

BLOCKED: raw `op inject -o <plugin-data>/.env` overwrites the whole file and DROPS local-only secrets (ACE_WEB_PAT_TOKEN and any key not in .env.tpl). Use `/ace:setup --force-env` (= `bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/bin/ace-setup" --force-env`), which snapshots the `# --- ACE local-only secrets ---` block and re-appends it after the inject. (A raw inject to a NON-plugin-data path, e.g. a dev-worktree ./.env, is not blocked.)
